_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder

title: Community Solution: CODE
subtitle: create solutions with coding!
overview: This session introduces students to how coding can be used to solve real-world community problems. Students explore the CODE framework — Create, Organize, Develop, Empower — while learning basic programming logic, teamwork, and digital innovation.

images._note: Add photos from coding activities, app design sketches, teamwork sessions, and student project demos.

gallery.caption: Coding helps transform ideas into digital solutions

gallery._placeholder: ADD: App or website design sketch photo
gallery.caption: Apps can solve real community challenges
Technology projects grow stronger with teamwork

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Scratch or block coding project image
gallery.caption: Block coding makes programming fun and beginner-friendly

keyTopics: C — Create: Build new ideas using technology
O — Organize: Plan and structure coding projects
D — Develop: Write and improve programs
E — Empower: Use technology to help communities
Apps & Websites — Digital solutions for society
Collaboration — Working together in tech teams

activity.title: Design a Community Helper App
steps: Identify a community problem students face
Discuss how technology could help solve it
Sketch a simple app or website idea
Create a short presentation explaining the solution
Present the app idea to the class

quiz.q: What does the 'C' in CODE stand for?
quiz.explanation: C stands for Create — using coding to build new ideas and solutions.
Coding means giving instructions that computers can understand and follow.
Algorithms are clear step-by-step instructions used to solve problems.

quiz.q: Which coding platform uses blocks for beginners?
quiz.explanation: Scratch uses visual coding blocks to make programming easy for beginners.

options: It helps solve problems using technology
quiz.explanation: Coding helps build apps, websites, and tools that solve real-world problems.

quiz.q: What does the 'O' in CODE stand for?
quiz.explanation: O stands for Organize — planning and structuring projects carefully.

quiz.q: What does the 'D' in CODE stand for?
quiz.explanation: D stands for Develop — building and improving programs.

quiz.q: What does the 'E' in CODE stand for?
quiz.explanation: E stands for Empower — using technology to help people and communities.

quiz.q: Which of these can be created using coding?
quiz.explanation: Coding is used to create apps, websites, games, and many digital tools.

quiz.q: Why is teamwork useful in coding projects?
quiz.explanation: Technology projects often require teamwork and collaboration.
